content_summary: This Goldman Sachs report analyzes emerging market food inflation drivers, distinguishing between global, regional, and local factors across 125 countries. It demonstrates that EM food inflation is structurally more volatile than DM inflation due to higher food weights in CPI baskets and supply-chain vulnerabilities, and projects upward pressure of approximately 2 percentage points on global food inflation in H2 2026 driven by soft commodities, fertilizers, diesel prices, and USD strength, with heightened vulnerability in countries like Ethiopia, Pakistan, and Senegal.
